audit_items.proof_trace: In `ldap_url_parsehosts`, when processing a bracket-format IPv6 host (e.g., `[::1]`), `LDAP_STRDUP(ludp->lud_host+1)` at line 1396 can return NULL on OOM; the result is unconditionally assigned to `ludp->lud_host` at line 1399 without a NULL check. The subsequent `strchr(ludp->lud_host, ']')` at line 1400 then dereferences the potentially-NULL pointer — the guard at line 1401 checks strchr's return value, not the input. The identical pattern at line 1432 (`ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(ludp->lud_host)`) is similarly unguarded.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker-controlled LDAP server address containing a bracket-IPv6 notation (e.g., `[::1]`) is passed to `ldap_url_parsehosts`; under memory pressure the missing post-STRDUP NULL check causes a NULL dereference in `strchr` at L1400, crashing the process (DoS).
